content:
# Competitor Audit Template

**Tool ID:** T038-02
**Block:** B038 (Experience Innovation & Differentiation)
**Domain:** Customer Experience & Service (D02_customer_experience)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 30-45 minutes per competitor visit
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. Know your competition not to copy them, but to understand where you are genuinely different -- and where you need to be.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Manager working in Customer Experience & Service.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Structured template for auditing competitor cafes covering coffee quality, food offering, atmosphere, service style, pricing, unique features, and identified gaps or opportunities. Visit 2-3 competitors per quarter.

This tool supports **Experience Innovation & Differentiation** (B038) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager (or Owner)
**Time Required:** 30-45 minutes per competitor visit (plus 15 min write-up)
**Frequency:** Quarterly -- audit 2-3 competitors per cycle. Revisit top competitor annually.

**What You Will Need:**
- This template (on your phone or printed discreetly)
- EUR 10-15 for purchases (coffee + food item)
- Phone for discreet photos (exterior, menu board, presentation)
- Notebook for on-the-spot observations

**Steps:**
1. Visit as a normal customer -- do not announce yourself
2. Order an espresso-based drink and one food item
3. Observe and record everything using the sections below
4. Note what they do BETTER than you and what you do BETTER than them
5. Complete the summary and share with Owner within 48 hours
6. Transfer insights to T038-03 (Innovation Pipeline) for action

---

## Checklist

### Visit Details

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Competitor name | |
| Location | |
| Date and time of visit | |
| Day of week | |
| Auditor | |
| Visit type | First visit / Return visit |

### Section 1: First Impression (Score 1-5)

| # | Area | Score (1-5) | Notes | Better Than Us? |
|---|------|-------------|-------|----------------|
| 1.1 | Exterior appearance and signage | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 1.2 | Ease of finding / visibility from street | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 1.3 | Window display and kerb appeal | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 1.4 | Entrance experience (welcome, cleanliness) | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 1.5 | Overall "feel" within 10 seconds | | | Yes / No / Same |

**Section 1 average:** _____ / 5

### Section 2: Coffee Quality (Score 1-5)

| # | Area | Score (1-5) | Notes | Better Than Us? |
|---|------|-------------|-------|----------------|
| 2.1 | Drink ordered: _____ Price: EUR _____ | | | |
| 2.2 | Espresso quality (crema, flavour, balance) | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 2.3 | Milk texture and temperature | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 2.4 | Presentation / latte art | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 2.5 | Coffee bean source / roaster displayed? | | Which: _____ | Yes / No / Same |
| 2.6 | Alternative milk options available | | Which: _____ | Yes / No / Same |
| 2.7 | Brew methods beyond espresso | | Which: _____ | Yes / No / Same |

**Section 2 average:** _____ / 5

### Section 3: Food Offering (Score 1-5)

| # | Area | Score (1-5) | Notes | Better Than Us? |
|---|------|-------------|-------|----------------|
| 3.1 | Food item ordered: _____ Price: EUR _____ | | | |
| 3.2 | Menu range and variety | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 3.3 | Food quality and freshness | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 3.4 | Presentation and plating | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 3.5 | Dietary options (vegan, gluten-free, etc.) | | Which: _____ | Yes / No / Same |
| 3.6 | Allergen information availability | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 3.7 | Made in-house vs bought in | | | Yes / No / Same |

**Section 3 average:** _____ / 5

### Section 4: Service (Score 1-5)

| # | Area | Score (1-5) | Notes | Better Than Us? |
|---|------|-------------|-------|----------------|
| 4.1 | Greeting within 30 seconds of entering | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 4.2 | Staff knowledge (could answer questions about menu) | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 4.3 | Speed of service (order to receipt): _____ min | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 4.4 | Friendliness and warmth | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 4.5 | Proactive engagement (recommendations, check-back) | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 4.6 | Payment process (smooth, options available) | | | Yes / No / Same |

**Section 4 average:** _____ / 5

### Section 5: Atmosphere and Environment (Score 1-5)

| # | Area | Score (1-5) | Notes | Better Than Us? |
|---|------|-------------|-------|----------------|
| 5.1 | Interior design and aesthetic | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 5.2 | Seating comfort and variety | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 5.3 | Music (appropriate volume and style) | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 5.4 | Lighting (natural and artificial) | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 5.5 | Cleanliness of tables, floor, toilets | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 5.6 | Wi-Fi and power sockets for laptops | | | Yes / No / Same |
| 5.7 | Outdoor seating | | | Yes / No / Same |

**Section 5 average:** _____ / 5

### Section 6: Pricing and Value

| Item | Their Price (EUR) | Our Price (EUR) | Difference | Notes |
|------|-------------------|-----------------|------------|-------|
| Espresso | | | | |
| Cappuccino / Flat White | | | | |
| Filter / Batch brew | | | | |
| Croissant / Pastry | | | | |
| Sandwich / Main food item | | | | |
| Alternative milk surcharge | | | | |

### Section 7: Unique Differentiators

**What do they do that we do NOT do?**
1. _____
2. _____
3. _____

**What do WE do that they do NOT do?**
1. _____
2. _____
3. _____

**Their single biggest strength:** _____

**Their single biggest weakness:** _____

### Overall Summary

| Section | Their Score (avg) | Our Estimated Score | Gap |
|---------|-------------------|---------------------|-----|
| 1. First Impression | | | |
| 2. Coffee Quality | | | |
| 3. Food Offering | | | |
| 4. Service | | | |
| 5. Atmosphere | | | |
| **Overall Average** | | | |

**Key takeaways:**
1. _____
2. _____
3. _____

**Actions for our cafe:**
1. _____
2. _____

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Copying Instead of Differentiating

**Why it happens:** You see a competitor doing something well and immediately want to replicate it. But copying makes you a follower, not a leader.
**How to prevent:** For every competitor strength you identify, ask: "How can we solve the same customer need in OUR unique way?" Focus on gaps they are not filling.
**If it is already happening:** Review your innovation pipeline (T038-03). Remove any items that are pure copies. Replace with ideas that build on your own identity.

### Risk 2: Only Auditing Direct Competitors

**Why it happens:** You visit the cafe across the street but ignore the bakery, the juice bar, or the co-working space with coffee. Your customers choose between all of them.
**How to prevent:** Audit at least one indirect competitor per quarter. Where else do your target customers spend EUR 3-5 on a drink?
**If it is already happening:** Add a non-cafe competitor to your next audit cycle.

### Risk 3: Audit Becomes a One-Off Exercise

**Why it happens:** Manager visits a competitor once, writes notes, and never goes back. The competitive landscape changes but your understanding does not.
**How to prevent:** Schedule quarterly competitor visits in the calendar. Rotate which competitors you visit. Track changes over time.
**If it is already happening:** Book your next 2 competitor visits now. Put them in the calendar with reminders.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Focus heavily on Section 2 (Coffee Quality) and Section 7 (Differentiators). Your coffee programme is your primary competitive advantage.
**Chain / Franchise:** Compare against both local independents and other chain locations. What does your brand offer that independents cannot?
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Simplify to Sections 2, 4, and 6. Your competitors are grab-and-go -- speed and quality are everything.
**Bakery Cafe:** Expand Section 3 significantly. Compare baking quality, range, freshness, and display presentation in detail.

**Making it yours:**
- Keep a competitor file with all completed audits for trend comparison
- Share insights (not scores) with the team -- they often spot things customers notice
- Use findings to inform seasonal changes, menu updates, and experience improvements
- Never disparage competitors to customers -- compete through excellence, not criticism

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T038-01 -- Differentiator Discovery Workshop Guide (L1, template)
- T038-03 -- Innovation Pipeline Tracker (L2, tracker)
- T038-04 -- Seasonal Experience Calendar Template (L2, template)
- T038-05 -- Experience Performance Dashboard (L3, dashboard)

**Related blocks:**
- B036
- B037

**Parent block:** B038 -- Experience Innovation & Differentiation
**Module:** M02: Customer Journey & Retention
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
